Who Gets the Credit?






Corrine

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
The Problem

A customer sees a Facebook ad →
searches on Google →
clicks an email →
buys.


Who gets the credit for that conversion?


The answer depends entirely on which attribution model you choose.

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
5 Attribution Models



Model
Logic




First-touch
100% credit → first touchpoint


Last-touch
100% credit → last touchpoint


Linear
Credit split equally across all touches


Time Decay
More credit to touches closer to conversion


Position-based
40% first · 20% middle · 40% last




Same customer journey. Five different answers.

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
Visualizing the Difference
One journey: Facebook → Google → Email → Purchase

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
The Numbers Can Diverge Drastically
Imagine 1,000 conversions across Facebook, Google, Email










Facebook credit: 50 → 420 depending on model chosen.

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
Which Model Should You Use?


Business Goal → Model

🎯 Brand awareness → First-touch

💰 Direct conversion → Last-touch

⚖️ Balanced view → Linear

🕐 Recency matters → Time Decay

🔁 Nurture + close → Position-based



Advanced: Spend-Weighted
Weight credit not just by position,
but by how much was spent
at each touchpoint.

Channels that cost more
get held to a higher standard.

title: Who Gets the Credit?
text:
Takeaway

Attribution isn’t a statistics problem.
It’s a business decision.


The model you choose determines which channels
get budget next quarter.
Choose deliberately.
